title: Senior Backend Engineer
education: Bachelor's in Computer Science or related field
description: Build and scale payment infrastructure handling millions of transactions per day. You will design RESTful APIs using FastAPI and Python, manage PostgreSQL and Redis data layers, and deploy services on AWS using Docker and Kubernetes. Deep understanding of distributed systems and high-availability architecture required.

title: Machine Learning Engineer
education: Master's or PhD in Machine Learning, AI, or related field
description: Research and implement state-of-the-art machine learning models for real-world applications. Work with PyTorch and TensorFlow to train large-scale models, use scikit-learn for classical ML pipelines, and leverage Pandas and NumPy for data processing. Experience with distributed training and MLOps tools preferred.

title: Full Stack Developer
education: Bachelor's in Computer Science or equivalent experience
description: Design and build consumer-facing e-commerce features used by millions of merchants worldwide. You will develop React and Next.js frontends with TypeScript, build Node.js backends exposing GraphQL APIs, and work with PostgreSQL for data persistence. Comfort with both UI and infrastructure is essential.

title: Data Engineer
education: Bachelor's in Computer Science, Data Science, or Engineering
description: Build and maintain reliable data pipelines that power business intelligence and machine learning across the company. Implement Spark jobs for large-scale data transformation, orchestrate workflows with Airflow, model data with dbt, and store results in PostgreSQL. Strong Python and SQL skills are a must.

title: DevOps / Platform Engineer
education: Bachelor's in Computer Science or equivalent
description: Own the infrastructure that runs GitHub for millions of developers. Design and automate cloud infrastructure using Terraform on AWS, manage container orchestration with Kubernetes and Helm, implement CI/CD workflows with GitHub Actions, and ensure reliability through Prometheus and Grafana monitoring.

title: Frontend Engineer
education: Bachelor's in Computer Science or related field
description: Build the web interface for a collaborative design tool used by millions. Deep TypeScript and React expertise required. You will develop highly interactive UI components with Next.js, consume GraphQL and REST APIs, and obsess over performance and accessibility. Experience with rendering pipelines is a plus.

title: Data Scientist
education: Master's or PhD in Statistics, Mathematics, or Computer Science
description: Develop recommendation models, run A/B experiments, and generate insights that shape the Netflix product. Use Python and R for statistical modelling, scikit-learn for predictive models, Pandas and NumPy for data wrangling, and Spark for large-scale analysis. Strong statistical fundamentals required.

title: Cloud Solutions Architect
education: Bachelor's in Computer Science or Engineering
description: Architect multi-cloud solutions for enterprise clients managing global traffic at scale. Design infrastructure across AWS and GCP using Terraform and Ansible, orchestrate services with Kubernetes, and build automation tooling in Python. Must be able to assess trade-offs across cost, latency, and resilience.

title: Backend Engineer – Go
education: Bachelor's in Computer Science or equivalent
description: Build high-throughput microservices for Uber's core ride and delivery platform. Primary language is Go; Python used for tooling and data pipelines. Services communicate over gRPC, use PostgreSQL and Redis for storage, and Kafka for event streaming. Deep knowledge of concurrency and low-latency design essential.

title: MLOps Engineer
education: Bachelor's or Master's in Computer Science or Machine Learning
description: Bridge the gap between research and production at the world's largest open-source AI platform. Deploy and monitor ML models using MLflow and Docker, orchestrate training pipelines with Airflow and Ray, manage model serving infrastructure on Kubernetes and AWS, and contribute to open-source tooling in Python and PyTorch.
